message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: I own the NDCG IDCG fix in metrics.py. Please list your files so we can confirm disjoint ownership.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: My task: add graded relevance support (relevance_scores param) to NDCG metric in metrics.py. What files do you need?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both must touch NDCG.compute in the same file - unavoidable overlap. I will scope my change narrowly to the IDCG calculation (lines ~380-386: idcg computed via range(1,len(expected_ids)+1) instead of min(retrieved,expected)) and leave the compute() signature/discounted_gain calls otherwise untouched so your relevance_scores param addition merges cleanly on top. I won't touch retrieved/expected texts handling or add new params. Will merge your branch in before final patch.txt per protocol.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both need to touch NDCG in the same file/class — true file-level split isn't possible. My feature adds an optional relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute() (used for both discounted gain and ideal gain, default binary 1.0 preserved). Can you describe exactly what your IDCG fix changes (which lines/logic)? Proposal: you finish+push your IDCG fix first, then I merge team/agent1 into my branch and layer the relevance_scores param on top so ideal gain also honors it. That avoids duplicate/conflicting edits.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Agreed. You scope to IDCG range bugfix only; I'll add relevance_scores param to compute() (default binary 1.0), using it for both dcg and idcg gain lookups. Will merge team/agent1 into my branch before finalizing patch.txt.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Agreed - I'll implement+commit+push first, you merge team/agent1 on top. Exact change: in NDCG.compute(), replace the 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' line + its comment with 'ideal_length = len(expected_ids)' (drop the min-with-retrieved logic), keeping the idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, ideal_length+1)) loop as-is. No other lines in NDCG touched, compute() signature unchanged. Will message DONE once pushed.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Pushed to team/agent1 (commit e9fe95aae). Only change: ideal_length = len(expected_ids) replacing the min(...) line, comment updated. Nothing else in NDCG touched. Go ahead and merge/layer your relevance_scores param on top.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Implemented relevance_scores param on NDCG.compute() — used for both dcg gain lookup and idcg ideal-ordering (sorted descending), default 1.0 preserves old binary behavior. All 42 existing tests in tests/evaluation/test_metrics.py pass. Ready to merge your branch in before finalizing patch.txt — let me know when your IDCG fix is pushed.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into my branch, resolved the IDCG conflict by combining your ideal_length=len(expected_ids) fix with my ideal_relevances sorting. All good except one pre-existing test now fails due to your IDCG behavior change: test_metrics.py::test_ndcg Case 4 (expected_ids=[id1,id2,id3,id4], retrieved_ids=[id1,id2], mode=linear) expects 1.0 but now gets 0.6367, since IDCG is no longer capped by len(retrieved_ids). This is a consequence of your bugfix, not mine — do you want to update that test's expected value, or should I? Let me know so we don't duplicate edits to test_metrics.py.
